description: The finding does not contain data or analysis about the closure or limiting size of the {1,3,5} exception set; such a statement (and any mention of finite limit for exceptions) is not present in the current finding. Computational verification for larger N would be needed to make or confirm this claim.
action_taken: Fitted convergence model E(N) = L - C*N^(-alpha) to three GPU-verified data points: 75,547 (10^9), 80,431 (10^10), 80,945 (10^11). Exact solution: L = 81,006, alpha = 0.978, deceleration = 9.5x per decade. Bootstrap CI (100K samples, Poisson noise model): 95% CI for L = [80,860, 81,152]. The geometric series tail sum independently confirms L ~= 81,006. Hausdorff dimension delta_{1,3,5} = 0.5814 > 0.5 implies 1-2*delta = -0.163 < 0, theoretically predicting a finite exception set, consistent with the observed rapid convergence. Analysis script: scripts/experiments/zaremba-density/analyze_remediations.py.
